title: 1.1: Tests that messages from one sender domain are routed to that domain's configured target
1.2: Tests that messages from a different sender domain are routed to that domain's different configured target
1.3: Tests that each target receives correct count of messages (no duplication or cross-routing)
1.4: Tests that allowed senders can successfully deliver messages
1.5: Tests that senders matching a domain rejection rule are rejected with error
1.6: Tests that senders not matching any rule are rejected via default rejection
1.7: Tests that postmaster address matching is case-insensitive
1.8: Tests that full email address matching is case-insensitive
1.9: Tests that domain-only source matching is case-insensitive
2.1: Places the domain routing test in internal/msgpipeline/
2.2: Places the source rejection test in internal/msgpipeline/
2.3: Places the case-insensitive matching test in internal/msgpipeline/
3.1: All tests use Go standard testing framework with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow Go Test naming convention with Test prefix
3.3: All tests use standard Go testing assertion methods
4.1: Tests configure MsgPipeline with perSource map for sender routing
4.2: Tests verify that targets received the expected messages
